########################################################
# ACCESSING MOBILITY & TRANSPORT EQUITY ANALYSIS
# Author: Nasteho Ahmed
# Data: CleanData (Numbers export)
########################################################

# -----------------------------
# 1. LOAD LIBRARIES
# -----------------------------
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(readr)
library(tidyverse)
library(MASS)
library(caret)

# -----------------------------
# 2. LOAD DATA (NUMBERS EXPORT)
# -----------------------------
walkdata <- read_csv("finalcleanwalk.csv")  
# If still Numbers export, convert first to CSV

# -----------------------------
# 3. CLEAN COLUMN NAMES
# -----------------------------
colnames(walkdata) <- gsub("[, ]", "", colnames(walkdata))

# -----------------------------
# 4. CLEAN DATA TYPES
# -----------------------------
walkdata <- walkdata %>%
  mutate(
    AGE = as.numeric(AGE),
    ZINC2 = as.numeric(ZINC2),
    RACE = as.factor(RACE),
    METRO3 = as.factor(METRO3),
    WBBIKELANE = as.factor(WBBIKELANE),
    WBBIKEWALK = as.character(WBBIKEWALK)
  )

# -----------------------------
# 5. CREATE DEPENDENT VARIABLE
# -----------------------------
walkdata <- walkdata %>%
  mutate(
    walkbikedummy = ifelse(WBBIKEWALK %in% c("1","2","3"), 1, 0)
  )

table(walkdata$walkbikedummy)

# -----------------------------
# 6. CREATE AGE GROUPS
# -----------------------------
walkdata <- walkdata %>%
  mutate(AgeGroup = case_when(
    AGE < 15 ~ "0-14",
    AGE < 30 ~ "15-29",
    AGE < 45 ~ "30-44",
    AGE < 60 ~ "45-59",
    AGE < 75 ~ "60-74",
    TRUE ~ "75+"
  ))

# -----------------------------
# 7. DESCRIPTIVE ANALYSIS
# -----------------------------

# Race distribution
ggplot(walkdata, aes(x = RACE)) +
  geom_bar(fill = "steelblue") +
  theme_minimal()

# Age distribution
ggplot(walkdata, aes(x = AgeGroup)) +
  geom_bar(fill = "darkgreen") +
  theme_minimal()

# -----------------------------
# 8. DISPROPORTIONALITY INDEX
# -----------------------------
disprop <- walkdata %>%
  group_by(RACE) %>%
  summarise(
    total = n(),
    walkers = sum(walkbikedummy, na.rm = TRUE),
    observed = walkers / sum(walkers),
    expected = total / nrow(walkdata),
    index = observed / expected
  )

ggplot(disprop, aes(x = RACE, y = index)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "purple") +
  theme_minimal()

# -----------------------------
# 9. BASELINE LOGISTIC MODEL
# -----------------------------
model1 <- glm(
  walkbikedummy ~ AGE + ZINC2 + RACE + METRO3,
  data = walkdata,
  family = binomial
)

summary(model1)
AIC(model1)

# -----------------------------
# 10. INTERACTION MODELS
# -----------------------------

# Race x income
model2 <- glm(
  walkbikedummy ~ AGE + ZINC2 * RACE + METRO3,
  data = walkdata,
  family = binomial
)

summary(model2)

# Metro x income
model3 <- glm(
  walkbikedummy ~ AGE + ZINC2 * METRO3 + RACE,
  data = walkdata,
  family = binomial
)

summary(model3)

# Bike lane effects
model4 <- glm(
  walkbikedummy ~ AGE + ZINC2 * WBBIKELANE + RACE + METRO3,
  data = walkdata,
  family = binomial
)

summary(model4)

# -----------------------------
# 11. PREDICTIONS
# -----------------------------
walkdata$predicted_prob <- predict(model1, type = "response")

walkdata$pred_class <- ifelse(walkdata$predicted_prob > 0.5, 1, 0)

head(walkdata$predicted_prob)

# Example individual prediction
new_person <- data.frame(
  AGE = 62,
  ZINC2 = 16021,
  RACE = "01",
  METRO3 = "3"
)

predict(model1, newdata = new_person, type = "response")

# -----------------------------
# 12. MODEL COMPARISON
# -----------------------------
AIC(model1, model2, model3, model4)

# -----------------------------
# 13. VISUALIZATION OF PREDICTIONS
# -----------------------------
ggplot(walkdata, aes(x = AGE, y = predicted_prob)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.3) +
  geom_smooth() +
  theme_minimal()

# -----------------------------
# END OF ANALYSIS
# -----------------------------
